title: Adverse Event Analysis — Summary
text: This sub-project of the HARMONY Terlipressin Registry analyzes adverse events (AE) associated with terlipressin treatment in patients with hepatorenal syndrome-acute kidney injury (HRS-AKI) across 15 hospitals.

title: Adverse Event Analysis — Summary
text: This sub-project of the HARMONY Terlipressin Registry analyzes adverse events (AE) associated with terlipressin treatment in patients with hepatorenal syndrome-acute kidney injury (HRS-AKI) across 15 hospitals.

title: Adverse Event Analysis — Summary
text:
Data Pipeline





flowchart TD
    A[Parent Project: final_master_01282026.xlsx] --> B[Load & type-convert columns]
    B --> C[Derive AE variables]
    C --> D[Descriptive Tables]
    C --> E[Regression Models]
    C --> F[Subgroup Analyses]
    D --> G[CSV outputs in results/]
    E --> G
    F --> G

title: Adverse Event Analysis — Summary
text:
Adverse Event Definitions



Variable
Definition
Type




any_ae
Any adverse event
Categorical


abdominal_ae
abdpain OR diarrhea OR nausea
Categorical


serious_ae
respfail OR brady OR terli_7 OR terli_8
Categorical


terli_ae_abdpain
Abdominal pain
Categorical


terli_ae_nausea
Nausea
Categorical


terli_ae_diarrhea
Diarrhea
Categorical


terli_ae_volover
Volume overload
Categorical


terli_ae_brady
Bradycardia
Categorical


terli_ae_respfail
Respiratory failure
Categorical


terli_7
Ischemic event 1
Categorical


terli_8
Ischemic event 2
Categorical

title: Adverse Event Analysis — Summary
text:
Other Key Derived Variables







Variable
Formula




BMI
aki_weight / (aki_height/100)^2


meld_fda
1 if coalesce(day0_meld, admit_meld, admit_meld_3) > 35


rrt_90days
1 if days_to_rrt <= 90


lt_90days
1 if time_to_transplant <= 90

title: Adverse Event Analysis — Summary
text:
Analysis Summary

1. AE Incidence by Extended Criteria (Tables)
Stratified AE rates comparing standard vs. extended terlipressin criteria:



Table
Stratification Variable




table1_criteria_2.csv
ACLF criteria (criteria_2)


table1_criteria_3.csv
sCr criteria (criteria_3)


table1_criteria_4.csv
Hypoxemia criteria (criteria_4)





2. Baseline Characteristics by AE Type (Tables)
Full baseline characteristics stratified by AE occurrence:







Table
Column Variable




table1_any_ae.csv
Any AE (any_ae)


table1_abdominal_ae.csv
Abdominal AE (abdominal_ae)


table1_serious_ae.csv
Serious AE (serious_ae)


table1_terli_ae_respfail.csv
Respiratory failure (terli_ae_respfail)





2b. AE Cross-Tabulations (04/07/2026 Update)
New variables added to stratified tables:







Variable
Definition




comb_effusion_edema
4-level: 0=neither, 1=effusion only, 2=edema only, 3=both


ratio_albumin_b4terli
totalalbumin_before_terli / aki_weight


ratio_albumin_total
total_albumin / aki_weight



New tables: serious_ae and terli_ae_respfail as column variables with AE cross-tabs.






Note



Coding fix applied: For patients with chest_imaging=1, missing chest_imaging_effusion and chest_imaging_pulmedema are set to 0.




3. Logistic Regression — Predictors of AE

Univariable: scr_terli_day0, respiratory_f, aclf_grade → outcome any_ae
Multivariable (original): aclf_grade + scr_terli_day0 + respiratory_f → outcome any_ae
Multivariable (04/07/2026 update): sex_male + inr_admit + akin + chest_imaging_effusion → outcome any_ae



4. Competing Risk Models — 90-Day Mortality
Univariable (Fine-Gray, failcode=1 death): All individual AE types + serious_ae
Multivariable (4 models):







Model
Covariates




1
any_ae + age + race + hispanic + admit_meld_3 (updated 04/07/2026, was day0_meld)


2
any_ae + age + race + hispanic


3
serious_ae + age + race + hispanic + admit_meld_3


4
serious_ae + age + race + hispanic





5. Subgroup: ICU Admission

Cohort: location_terli_day0 == 1 OR 2
Model: Univariable logistic — any_ae → icu_admit



6. Subgroup: Transplant-Listed Patients

Cohort: listed_transplant == 1
Model: Univariable competing risk (failcode=2, transplant)
Covariates: any_ae, serious_ae, hrs_responders_cat_2, day0_meld, aclf_grade, meld_fda

title: Results
text:
AE rates stratified by ACLF (criteria_2), sCr (criteria_3), and Hypoxemia (criteria_4) criteria.


Code
ae_vars <- c("any_ae", "terli_ae_abdpain", "terli_ae_nausea", "terli_ae_diarrhea",
             "terli_ae_volover", "terli_ae_brady", "terli_7", "terli_8", "terli_ae_respfail",
             "serious_ae", "comb_effusion_edema", "ratio_albumin_b4terli", "ratio_albumin_total")

ae_cat_vars <- c("any_ae", "terli_ae_abdpain", "terli_ae_nausea", "terli_ae_diarrhea",
                 "terli_ae_volover", "terli_ae_brady", "terli_7", "terli_8", "terli_ae_respfail",
                 "serious_ae", "comb_effusion_edema")

exact_vars <- c("terli_ae_abdpain", "terli_ae_nausea", "terli_ae_diarrhea",
                "terli_ae_volover", "terli_ae_brady", "terli_7", "terli_8")

for (crit in c("criteria_2", "criteria_3", "criteria_4")) {
  create_table_one(
    all_vars = ae_vars, cat_vars = ae_cat_vars,
    data = final_master, exact = exact_vars,
    strata = crit,
    output = paste0('/Users/to909/Desktop/Terlipressin projects/Adverse event/results/table1_', crit, '.csv')
  )
}

title: Results
section: 4. Logistic Regression — Predictors of Any AE
text:
4. Logistic Regression — Predictors of Any AE

Univariable


Code
final_master <- final_master %>% mutate_at(c("aclf_grade", "respiratory_f"), as.factor)

final_master %>%
  select(any_ae, scr_terli_day0, respiratory_f, aclf_grade) %>%
  tbl_uvregression(
    method = glm,
    method.args = list(family = binomial),
    y = any_ae,
    pvalue_fun = ~style_pvalue(.x, digits = 3),
    exponentiate = TRUE
  ) %>%
  modify_caption("**Univariable logistic regression for any adverse event**") %>%
  bold_p(0.05) %>%
  bold_labels() %>%
  add_n()

Multivariable (04/07/2026 Update): sex_male + inr_admit + akin + chest_imaging_effusion


Code
final_master <- final_master %>% mutate_at(c("akin"), as.factor)

glm(any_ae ~ sex_male + inr_admit + akin + chest_imaging_effusion,
    family = binomial, data = final_master) %>%
  tbl_regression(exponentiate = TRUE) %>%
  add_n() %>%
  bold_p(0.05) %>%
  bold_labels() %>%
  modify_caption("**Multivariable logistic regression for any adverse event (updated)**")

title: Results
text:
5. Competing Risk Models — 90-Day Mortality

Univariable (Fine-Gray, failcode=1: death)


Code
final_master %>%
  select(any_ae, terli_ae_abdpain, terli_ae_nausea, terli_ae_diarrhea,
         terli_ae_volover, terli_ae_brady, terli_7, terli_8,
         terli_ae_respfail, serious_ae,
         time_to_death_90days_cmp, time_to_death_status_90days_cmp) %>%
  tbl_uvregression(
    method = tidycmprsk::crr,
    method.args = list(failcode = 1, cencode = 0),
    y = Surv(time_to_death_90days_cmp, time_to_death_status_90days_cmp),
    pvalue_fun = ~style_pvalue(.x, digits = 3),

title: Results
text:
6. Subgroup: ICU Admission
Cohort: patients with location_terli_day0 == 1 or 2 (floor or step-down at terlipressin initiation).


Code
final_master_location <- final_master %>%
  filter(location_terli_day0 == 1 | location_terli_day0 == 2)

final_master_location %>%
  select(icu_admit, any_ae) %>%
  tbl_uvregression(
    method = glm,
    method.args = list(family = binomial),
    y = icu_admit,
    pvalue_fun = ~style_pvalue(.x, digits = 3),
    exponentiate = TRUE
  ) %>%
  bold_p(0.05) %>%
  bold_labels() %>%
  add_n()

title: Results
text:
7. Subgroup: Transplant-Listed Patients
Cohort: listed_transplant == 1. Competing risk model with failcode=2 (transplant event).


Code
final_master_listed <- final_master %>% filter(listed_transplant == 1)

final_master_listed %>%
  select(any_ae, serious_ae, hrs_responders_cat_2, day0_meld, aclf_grade,
         meld_fda, time_to_death_90days_cmp, time_to_death_status_90days_cmp) %>%
  tbl_uvregression(
    method = tidycmprsk::crr,
    method.args = list(failcode = 2, cencode = 0),
    y = Surv(time_to_death_90days_cmp, time_to_death_status_90days_cmp),
    pvalue_fun = ~style_pvalue(.x, digits = 3),
    exponentiate = TRUE
  )
